steps.title: Express f(x+1) in terms of f(x)
steps.content:
For \(f(x)=15b^{x}\):
$$f(x+1)=15b^{x+1}=b \cdot 15b^{x}=b \cdot f(x)$$
So increasing \(x\) by 1 multiplies \(f(x)\) by \(b\).

steps.title: Convert the multiplicative factor to a percent increase
Match to choices
steps.content:
• A: \(1+\frac{b}{100}\) — does not equal \(100(b-1)\). Eliminated.
  • B: \(b+100\) — does not equal \(100(b-1)\). Eliminated.
  • C: \(100(b+1)\) — would equal \(100b+100\), not \(100b-100\). Eliminated.
  • D: \(100(b-1)\) — matches exactly. That matches choice D — D is correct.
